color_semantics.TEAL: quantum wavefunction, exponential decay tail, transmitted amplitude, tunneling
color_semantics.CRIMSON: classical reflection, forbidden region boundary, zero-transmission expectation

metadata.note: Card 05. Exclusions: no transmission-coefficient derivation, no WKB integral, no sinh² exact formula, no tunneling-time controversy.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.prompt: Fire a ball at a wall higher than its energy. It reflects — kinetic energy converts to potential, runs out, reverses. The wall wins, always.

beats.prompt: Classically, if kinetic energy is zero inside the barrier, there is no motion. The particle cannot be there. The wavefunction must be zero. But it isn't.

beats.prompt: Inside a forbidden region, Schrödinger's equation gives ψ'' = κ²ψ — a real exponential, not an oscillation. The wavefunction decays, but it does not reach zero.

beats.prompt: The mechanism: exponential decay through the barrier.

beats.prompt: That exponential sensitivity is not a flaw. It's the point.
